   456→class ChatWatcher:
   457→    """
   458→    Polls a specific Claude Code chat webview via UIA.
   459→    When a new response stabilises, either enqueues it for TTS (active session)
   460→    or holds it in _pending_queue and chimes (non-active session).
   461→    """
   462→    POLL_SECS   = 0.5
   463→    STABLE_SECS = 1.2
   464→
   465→    _STOP = {"Edit automatically", "Show command menu (/)",
   466→             "ctrl esc to focus or unfocus Claude", "Message input"}
   467→    _SKIP = {"Thinking", "Message actions", "Copy code to clipboard",
   468→             "Stop", "Regenerate", "tasks", "New session", "Ask before edits"}
   469→
   470→    def __init__(self, project_name: str = "", target_subname: str = ""):
   471→        self._chat_doc        = None
   472→        self._last_text              = ""
   473→        self._last_change            = 0.0
   474→        self._last_spoken            = ""
   475→        self._new_submission_pending = False  # True after user submits; cleared on _last_text change
   476→        self._pending_queue:  list[str]  = []
   477→        self._response_history: deque   = deque(maxlen=10)
   478→        self.project_name     = project_name
   479→        self._target_sub      = target_subname or VSCODE_TITLE
   480→
   481→    @property
   482→    def last_spoken(self) -> str:
   483→        return self._last_spoken
   484→
   485→    def flush_pending(self, tts_queue, loop) -> int:
   486→        items = self._pending_queue[:]
   487→        self._pending_queue.clear()
   488→        for text in items:
   489→            try:
   490→                asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(
   491→                    tts_queue.put((self.project_name, text)), loop
   492→                )
   493→            except Exception:
   494→                pass
   495→        return len(items)
   496→
   497→    # ── Find the webview ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   498→
   499→    def _find_webview(self):
   500→        vscode = auto.WindowControl(searchDepth=1, SubName=self._target_sub)
   501→        if not vscode.Exists(3):
   502→            return None
   503→        chrome = vscode.PaneControl(searchDepth=12,
   504→                                    ClassName="Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND")
   505→        if not chrome.Exists(2):
   506→            return None
   507→
   508→        docs: list[tuple[int, auto.Control]] = []
   509→
   510→        def collect(ctrl, d=0):
   511→            if d > 15:
   512→                return
   513→            try:
   514→                if ctrl.ControlTypeName == "DocumentControl":
   515→                    docs.append((d, ctrl))
   516→            except Exception:
   517→                pass
   518→            try:
   519→                child = ctrl.GetFirstChildControl()
   520→                while child:
   521→                    collect(child, d + 1)
   522→                    child = child.GetNextSiblingControl()
   523→            except Exception:
   524→                pass
   525→
   526→        collect(chrome)
   527→        unnamed = [(d, c) for d, c in docs if not (c.Name or "").strip()]
   528→        if unnamed:
   529→            return unnamed[-1][1]
   530→        return docs[-1][1] if docs else None
   531→
   532→    # ── Walk accessibility tree ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   533→
   534→    def _walk(self, ctrl, depth=0, max_depth=12, out=None):
   535→        if out is None:
   536→            out = []
   537→        if depth > max_depth:
   538→            return out
   539→        try:
   540→            name  = (ctrl.Name or "").strip()
   541→            ctype = ctrl.ControlTypeName or ""
   542→            if len(name) >= 4:
   543→                out.append((depth, ctype, name))
   544→        except Exception:
   545→            pass
   546→        try:
   547→            child = ctrl.GetFirstChildControl()
   548→            while child:
   549→                self._walk(child, depth + 1, max_depth, out)
   550→                child = child.GetNextSiblingControl()
   551→        except Exception:
   552→            pass
   553→        return out
   554→
   555→    # ── Extract latest Claude response ────────────────────────────────────────
   556→
   557→    def _extract_response(self, results):
   558→        """
   559→        Extract the latest Claude response.
   560→
   561→        Anchor strategy:
   562→          - "Message input" EditControl is a reliable end-of-chat marker.
   563→          - The last "Thinking" ButtonControl before it marks the start of
   564→            Claude's final response block (Thinking appears before each reply).
   565→          - Fallback: last "Message actions" before "Message input" if no Thinking.
   566→        """
   567→        # Find "Message input" EditControl position (end of chat content)
   568→        msg_input_pos = next(
   569→            (i for i, (_, ct, tx) in enumerate(results)
   570→             if ct == "EditControl" and tx == _CHAT_INPUT_HINT),
   571→            -1
   572→        )
   573→        if msg_input_pos == -1:
   574→            return ""
   575→
   576→        # Primary: last "Thinking" button before Message input
   577→        start = -1
   578→        for i, (_, ctype, text) in enumerate(results[:msg_input_pos]):
   579→            if ctype == "ButtonControl" and "Thinking" in text:
   580→                start = i
   581→
   582→        # Secondary: last "Message actions" before Message input
   583→        if start == -1:
   584→            for i, (_, ctype, text) in enumerate(results[:msg_input_pos]):
   585→                if ctype == "ButtonControl" and text == "Message actions":
   586→                    start = i
   587→
   588→        if start == -1:
   589→            return ""
   590→
   591→        parts: list[str] = []
   592→        seen:  set[str]  = set()
   593→
   594→        for _, ctype, text in results[start + 1: msg_input_pos]:
   595→            if text in self._STOP:
   596→                break
   597→            if ctype == "ButtonControl" and text == "Message actions":
   598→                break  # end of Claude's response block; user's next message follows
   599→            if text in self._SKIP or len(text) < 4:
   600→                continue
   601→            if ctype not in ("TextControl", "ListItemControl"):
   602→                continue
   603→            if text not in seen and not any(text in s for s in seen if len(s) > len(text)):
   604→                seen.add(text)
   605→                parts.append(text)
   606→
   607→        return " ".join(parts)
   608→
   609→    # ── Polling loop ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   610→
   611→    def start(self, tts_queue, loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop,
   612→              is_active_fn=None):
   613→        if is_active_fn is None:
   614→            is_active_fn = lambda: True
   615→
   616→        def poll():
   617→            while self._chat_doc is None:
   618→                self._chat_doc = self._find_webview()
   619→                if self._chat_doc is None:
   620→                    time.sleep(2)
   621→            label = f"[{self.project_name}] " if self.project_name else ""
   622→            print(f"[Cyrus] {label}Connected to Claude Code chat panel.")
   623→
   624→            # Seed: retry until we get non-empty content (avoids phantom read on startup)
   625→            for _ in range(6):
   626→                try:
   627→                    seed = self._extract_response(self._walk(self._chat_doc))
   628→                    if seed:
   629→                        break
   630→                except Exception:
   631→                    seed = ""
   632→                time.sleep(1.0)
   633→            self._last_spoken = seed
   634→            self._last_text   = seed
   635→            self._last_change = time.time()
   636→
   637→            while True:
   638→                time.sleep(self.POLL_SECS)
   639→                try:
   640→                    results  = self._walk(self._chat_doc)
   641→                    response = self._extract_response(results)
   642→                    now      = time.time()
   643→
   644→                    # Detect new user submission: count "Message actions" buttons
   645→                    msg_actions_count = sum(
   646→                        1 for _, ct, tx in results
   647→                        if ct == "ButtonControl" and tx == "Message actions"
   648→                    )
   649→                    if msg_actions_count != getattr(self, "_last_msg_actions_count", -1):
   650→                        prev = getattr(self, "_last_msg_actions_count", -1)
   651→                        self._last_msg_actions_count = msg_actions_count
   652→                        if prev != -1 and msg_actions_count > prev:
   653→                            self._new_submission_pending = True
   654→
   655→                    if response != self._last_text:
   656→                        self._last_text   = response
   657→                        self._last_change = now
   658→                        if response:
   659→                            print(f"[DBG {self.project_name}] chg: {response[:60]!r}")
   660→                        # _last_text changed → Claude started a new response.
   661→                        # If we have a pending submission, clear _last_spoken now
   662→                        # so the settled response fires TTS even if text is identical.
   663→                        if self._new_submission_pending:
   664→                            self._new_submission_pending = False
   665→                            self._last_spoken = ""
   666→                    elif response and response != self._last_spoken:
   667→                        # Time-based stability: fire once response unchanged for STABLE_SECS
   668→                        if now - self._last_change >= self.STABLE_SECS:
   669→                            self._last_spoken = response
   670→                            # Silently absorb if seed failed — never speak stale content
   671→                            if not seed:
   672→                                seed = response  # baseline set; next change will speak
   673→                                print(f"[DBG {self.project_name}] silent absorb")
   674→                                continue
   675→                            spoken = clean_for_speech(response)
   676→                            self._response_history.append(spoken)
   677→
   678→                            preview = spoken[:80] + ("..." if len(spoken) > 80 else "")
   679→                            print(f"\nCyrus [{self.project_name or 'Claude'}]: {preview}")
   680→
   681→                            if is_active_fn():
   682→                                print(f"[DBG {self.project_name}] → TTS queued")
   683→                                _tts_pending.set()
   684→                                asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(
   685→                                    tts_queue.put((self.project_name, spoken)), loop
   686→                                )
   687→                            else:
   688→                                print(f"[DBG {self.project_name}] → pending (active={_active_project!r})")
   689→                                self._pending_queue.append(spoken)
   690→                                play_chime()
   691→                                print(f"[queued: {self.project_name}] "
   692→                                      f"{len(self._pending_queue)} message(s) waiting")
   693→
   694→                except Exception:
   695→                    self._chat_doc = None
   696→                    while self._chat_doc is None:
   697→                        self._chat_doc = self._find_webview()
   698→                        if self._chat_doc is None:
   699→                            time.sleep(2)
   700→
   701→        threading.Thread(target=poll, daemon=True).start()
   702→

   704→# ── Permission Watcher ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   705→
   706→class PermissionWatcher:
   707→    """
   708→    Polls Claude Code's chat webview once per interval and checks for both:
   709→      1. Permission dialogs ("Yes, allow" buttons for bash commands)
   710→      2. Open-ended input prompts (EditControls with a label)
   711→    Single walk per poll — no extra thread needed for prompt detection.
   712→    """
   713→    POLL_SECS   = 0.3
   714→    ALLOW_WORDS = {"yes", "allow", "sure", "ok", "okay", "proceed", "yep", "yeah", "go"}
   715→    DENY_WORDS  = {"no", "deny", "cancel", "stop", "nope", "reject"}
   716→
   717→    _SKIP_PROMPT_NAMES  = {_CHAT_INPUT_HINT, ""}
   718→    _SKIP_PROMPT_LABELS = {"search", "find", "replace", "filter", "go to line"}
   719→
   720→    def __init__(self, project_name: str = "", target_subname: str = ""):
   721→        self._chat_doc  = None
   722→        # permission state
   723→        self._pending   = False
   724→        self._allow_btn = None
   725→        self._announced = ""
   726→        # input-prompt state
   727→        self._prompt_pending    = False
   728→        self._prompt_input_ctrl = None
   729→        self._prompt_announced  = ""
   730→        self.project_name = project_name
   731→        self._target_sub  = target_subname or VSCODE_TITLE
   732→
   733→    @property
   734→    def is_pending(self):
   735→        return self._pending
   736→
   737→    @property
   738→    def prompt_pending(self):
   739→        return self._prompt_pending
   740→
   741→    def _find_webview(self):
   742→        vscode = auto.WindowControl(searchDepth=1, SubName=self._target_sub)
   743→        if not vscode.Exists(3):
   744→            return None
   745→        chrome = vscode.PaneControl(searchDepth=12,
   746→                                    ClassName="Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND")
   747→        if not chrome.Exists(2):
   748→            return None
   749→        docs: list[tuple[int, auto.Control]] = []
   750→
   751→        def collect(ctrl, d=0):
   752→            if d > 15:
   753→                return
   754→            try:
   755→                if ctrl.ControlTypeName == "DocumentControl":
   756→                    docs.append((d, ctrl))
   757→            except Exception:
   758→                pass
   759→            try:
   760→                child = ctrl.GetFirstChildControl()
   761→                while child:
   762→                    collect(child, d + 1)
   763→                    child = child.GetNextSiblingControl()
   764→            except Exception:
   765→                pass
   766→
   767→        collect(chrome)
   768→        unnamed = [(d, c) for d, c in docs if not (c.Name or "").strip()]
   769→        if unnamed:
   770→            return unnamed[-1][1]
   771→        return docs[-1][1] if docs else None
   772→
   773→    def _scan(self):
   774→        """Walk the chat doc once; return (perm_btn, cmd, prompt_ctrl, prompt_label)."""
   775→        if not self._chat_doc:
   776→            return None, "", None, ""
   777→
   778→        items: list[tuple[int, str, str, object]] = []
   779→
   780→        def walk(ctrl, d=0):
   781→            if d > 12:
   782→                return
   783→            try:
   784→                name  = (ctrl.Name or "").strip()
   785→                ctype = ctrl.ControlTypeName or ""
   786→                if name:
   787→                    items.append((d, ctype, name, ctrl))
   788→            except Exception:
   789→                pass
   790→            try:
   791→                child = ctrl.GetFirstChildControl()
   792→                while child:
   793→                    walk(child, d + 1)
   794→                    child = child.GetNextSiblingControl()
   795→            except Exception:
   796→                pass
   797→
   798→        walk(self._chat_doc)
   799→
   800→        # ── Cache the chat input EditControl as a free side-effect ────────────
   801→        # This keeps _chat_input_cache warm so submit_to_vscode never calls
   802→        # _find_chat_input (which competes with our walks and times out).
   803→        if self.project_name not in _chat_input_cache:
   804→            for _, ctype, name, ctrl in items:
   805→                if ctype == "EditControl" and name == _CHAT_INPUT_HINT:
   806→                    _chat_input_cache[self.project_name] = ctrl
   807→                    break
   808→
   809→        # ── Permission button ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   810→        perm_btn, perm_cmd = None, ""
   811→        for i, (_, ctype, name, ctrl) in enumerate(items):
   812→            if ctype == "ButtonControl" and "Yes, allow" in name:
   813→                cmd = ""
   814→                hit_prompt = False
   815→                for j in range(max(0, i - 10), i):
   816→                    _, ct2, n2, _ = items[j]
   817→                    if "Allow this" in n2:
   818→                        hit_prompt = True
   819→                        continue
   820→                    if hit_prompt and ct2 == "TextControl" and len(n2) > 2:
   821→                        cmd = n2
   822→                        break
   823→                perm_btn, perm_cmd = ctrl, cmd
   824→                break
   825→
   826→        # ── Input prompt EditControl ───────────────────────────────────────────
   827→        prompt_ctrl, prompt_label = None, ""
   828→        for i, (_, ctype, name, ctrl) in enumerate(items):
   829→            if ctype != "EditControl":
   830→                continue
   831→            if name in self._SKIP_PROMPT_NAMES:
   832→                continue
   833→            label = ""
   834→            for j in range(max(0, i - 10), i):
   835→                _, ct2, n2, _ = items[j]
   836→                if ct2 not in ("TextControl", "StaticTextControl"):
   837→                    continue
   838→                n2s = n2.strip().lower()
   839→                if not n2s or n2s in self._SKIP_PROMPT_LABELS or len(n2) < 4:
   840→                    continue
   841→                label = n2.strip()
   842→                break
   843→            if label:
   844→                prompt_ctrl, prompt_label = ctrl, label
   845→                break
   846→
   847→        return perm_btn, perm_cmd, prompt_ctrl, prompt_label
   848→
   849→    def handle_response(self, text: str) -> bool:
   850→        if not self._pending or not self._allow_btn:
   851→            return False
   852→
   853→        words = set(text.lower().strip().split())
   854→
   855→        if words & self.ALLOW_WORDS:
   856→            print(f"[Cyrus] → Allowing command ({self.project_name or 'session'})")
   857→            try:
   858→                self._allow_btn.Click()
   859→            except Exception:
   860→                pass
   861→            self._pending   = False
   862→            self._allow_btn = None
   863→            return True
   864→
   865→        if words & self.DENY_WORDS:
   866→            print(f"[Cyrus] → Cancelling command ({self.project_name or 'session'})")
   867→            vscode = auto.WindowControl(searchDepth=1, SubName=self._target_sub)
   868→            if vscode.Exists(1):
   869→                try:
   870→                    vscode.SetFocus()
   871→                except Exception:
   872→                    pass
   873→            pyautogui.press("escape")
   874→            self._pending   = False
   875→            self._allow_btn = None
   876→            return True
   877→
   878→        return False
   879→
   880→    def handle_prompt_response(self, text: str) -> bool:
   881→        if not self._prompt_pending or not self._prompt_input_ctrl:
   882→            return False
   883→
   884→        cancel = {"cancel", "escape", "never mind", "nevermind", "stop", "dismiss", "close"}
   885→        if text.lower().strip() in cancel:
   886→            pyautogui.press("escape")
   887→            print(f"[Cyrus] → Dismissed prompt ({self.project_name or 'session'})")
   888→        else:
   889→            try:
   890→                self._prompt_input_ctrl.SetFocus()
   891→                time.sleep(0.05)
   892→                pyperclip.copy(text)
   893→                pyautogui.hotkey("ctrl", "a")
   894→                time.sleep(0.02)
   895→                pyautogui.hotkey("ctrl", "v")
   896→                time.sleep(0.05)
   897→                pyautogui.press("enter")
   898→                print(f"[Cyrus] → Prompt answered: {text!r} ({self.project_name or 'session'})")
   899→            except Exception as e:
   900→                print(f"[Cyrus] Prompt input error: {e}")
   901→
   902→        self._prompt_pending    = False
   903→        self._prompt_input_ctrl = None
   904→        return True
   905→
   906→    def start(self, loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop):
   907→        def poll():
   908→            while self._chat_doc is None:
   909→                self._chat_doc = self._find_webview()
   910→                if self._chat_doc is None:
   911→                    time.sleep(2)
   912→
   913→            while True:
   914→                time.sleep(self.POLL_SECS)
   915→                try:
   916→                    btn, cmd, p_ctrl, p_label = self._scan()
   917→
   918→                    # ── Permission button ──────────────────────────────────────
   919→                    if btn and cmd != self._announced:
   920→                        self._allow_btn  = btn
   921→                        self._pending    = True
   922→                        self._announced  = cmd
   923→                        prefix = f"In {self.project_name}: " if self.project_name else ""
   924→                        prompt = (f"{prefix}Claude wants to run: {cmd}. "
   925→                                  f"Say yes to allow or no to cancel.")
   926→                        print(f"\n[Permission] {prompt}")
   927→                        _stop_speech.set()
   928→                        asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(
   929→                            _speak_urgent(prompt), loop
   930→                        )
   931→                    elif not btn:
   932→                        self._announced = ""
   933→                        if self._pending:
   934→                            self._pending   = False
   935→                            self._allow_btn = None
   936→
   937→                    # ── Input prompt ───────────────────────────────────────────
   938→                    if p_ctrl and p_label != self._prompt_announced:
   939→                        self._prompt_input_ctrl = p_ctrl
   940→                        self._prompt_pending    = True
   941→                        self._prompt_announced  = p_label
   942→                        prefix = f"In {self.project_name}: " if self.project_name else ""
   943→                        prompt = f"{prefix}{p_label}"
   944→                        print(f"\n[Input Prompt] {prompt}")
   945→                        _stop_speech.set()
   946→                        asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(
   947→                            _speak_urgent(prompt), loop
   948→                        )
   949→                    elif not p_ctrl:
   950→                        self._prompt_announced = ""
   951→                        if self._prompt_pending:
   952→                            self._prompt_pending    = False
   953→                            self._prompt_input_ctrl = None
   954→
   955→                except Exception:
   956→                    self._chat_doc = None
   957→                    while self._chat_doc is None:
   958→                        self._chat_doc = self._find_webview()
   959→                        if self._chat_doc is None:
   960→                            time.sleep(2)
   961→
   962→        threading.Thread(target=poll, daemon=True).start()

  1000→    def rename_alias(self, old_alias: str, new_alias: str, proj: str) -> None:
  1001→        """Replace an auto-generated alias with a user-chosen name."""

  1005→    def _add_session(self, proj: str, subname: str, tts_queue, loop):
  1006→        global _whisper_prompt
  1007→        alias = _make_alias(proj)

  1010→        # Seed Whisper with project names so short words like "dev" are recognised
  1011→        names = " ".join(p for p in self._chat_watchers) + f" {proj}"
  1012→        _whisper_prompt = f"Cyrus, switch to {names.strip()}."
  1013→
  1014→        def is_active():

  1017→
  1018→        cw = ChatWatcher(project_name=proj, target_subname=subname)
  1019→        cw.start(tts_queue, loop, is_active_fn=is_active)
  1020→        self._chat_watchers[proj] = cw

  1026→    def start(self, loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop, tts_queue):

  1037→        try:

  1043→        if self.multi_session:

  1045→            print(f'[Cyrus] {len(self._chat_watchers)} sessions: {names}')

  1053→def vad_loop(on_utterance, loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop):
  1054→    model = load_silero_vad()
  1055→    ring: deque[tuple[bytes, bool]] = deque(maxlen=SPEECH_RING)
  1056→    recording     = False
  1057→    frames: list[bytes] = []
  1058→    silence_count = 0
  1059→    speech_frames = 0   # frames where speech was detected — drives adaptive silence
  1060→
  1061→    with sd.RawInputStream(samplerate=SAMPLE_RATE, channels=CHANNELS,
  1062→                           dtype="int16", blocksize=FRAME_SIZE) as stream:
  1063→        while not _shutdown.is_set():
  1064→            if _mic_muted.is_set() or _user_paused.is_set():
  1065→                stream.read(FRAME_SIZE)
  1066→                ring.clear()
  1067→                if recording:
  1068→                    frames.clear()
  1069→                    recording     = False
  1070→                    silence_count = 0
  1071→                time.sleep(0.01)
  1072→                continue
  1073→
  1074→            raw, _ = stream.read(FRAME_SIZE)
  1075→            frame_bytes = bytes(raw)
  1076→
  1077→            try:
  1078→                chunk = np.frombuffer(frame_bytes, dtype=np.int16).astype(np.float32) / 32768.0
  1079→                with torch.no_grad():
  1080→                    prob = model(torch.from_numpy(chunk), SAMPLE_RATE).item()
  1081→                is_speech = prob > SPEECH_THRESHOLD
  1082→            except Exception:
  1083→                continue
  1084→
  1085→            if not recording:
  1086→                ring.append((frame_bytes, is_speech))
  1087→                num_voiced = sum(1 for _, s in ring if s)
  1088→                if (len(ring) == ring.maxlen
  1089→                        and num_voiced / ring.maxlen >= SPEECH_RATIO):
  1090→                    recording     = True
  1091→                    frames        = [fb for fb, _ in ring]
  1092→                    silence_count = 0
  1093→                    speech_frames = 0
  1094→                    ring.clear()
  1095→                    print("Listening...", flush=True)
  1096→            else:
  1097→                frames.append(frame_bytes)
  1098→                if is_speech:
  1099→                    silence_count = 0
  1100→                    speech_frames += 1
  1101→                else:
  1102→                    silence_count += 1
  1103→
  1104→                # Adaptive silence: short pauses end quick commands fast;
  1105→                # longer utterances (>1.5 s of speech) get 2.5 s to think mid-sentence.
  1106→                adaptive_ring = (SILENCE_RING * 2
  1107→                                 if speech_frames * FRAME_MS > 1500
  1108→                                 else SILENCE_RING)
  1109→
  1110→                timed_out = len(frames) >= MAX_RECORD_FRAMES
  1111→                if silence_count >= adaptive_ring or timed_out:
  1112→                    if timed_out:
  1113→                        print(" [max duration]", flush=True)
  1114→                    raw_audio = b"".join(frames)
  1115→                    audio = (np.frombuffer(raw_audio, dtype=np.int16)
  1116→                               .astype(np.float32) / 32768.0)
  1117→                    recording     = False
  1118→                    frames        = []
  1119→                    silence_count = 0
  1120→                    speech_frames = 0
  1121→                    ring.clear()
  1122→                    model.reset_states()   # clear GRU state between utterances
  1123→                    loop.call_soon_threadsafe(on_utterance, audio)

  1126→# ── Speech-to-text ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1127→
  1128→# Phrases Whisper hallucinates on silence/noise (YouTube training data artefacts)
  1129→_HALLUCINATIONS = re.compile(
  1130→    r"thank(s| you) for watching"
  1131→    r"|see you (in the next|next time|again)"
  1132→    r"|don'?t forget to (like|subscribe)"
  1133→    r"|like and subscribe"
  1134→    r"|please subscribe"
  1135→    r"|subtitles by"
  1136→    r"|transcribed by",
  1137→    re.IGNORECASE,
  1138→)
  1139→
  1140→# RMS energy floor — skip transcription if audio is essentially silence
  1141→_MIN_RMS = 0.004   # ~-48 dBFS; tune up if hallucinations persist
  1142→
  1143→# Leading filler words to strip before forwarding to Claude Code
  1144→_FILLER_RE = re.compile(
  1145→    r"^(?:uh+|um+|er+|so|okay|ok|right|hey|please|can you|could you|would you)\s+",
  1146→    re.IGNORECASE,
  1147→)
  1148→
  1149→
  1150→def _strip_fillers(text: str) -> str:
  1151→    """Strip leading filler words — handles stacked fillers like 'uh um fix it'."""
  1152→    prev = None
  1153→    while prev != text:
  1154→        prev = text
  1155→        text = _FILLER_RE.sub("", text).strip()
  1156→    return text
  1157→
  1158→
  1159→def transcribe(whisper_model: WhisperModel, audio: np.ndarray) -> str:
  1160→    rms = float(np.sqrt(np.mean(audio ** 2)))
  1161→    if rms < _MIN_RMS:
  1162→        return ""   # too quiet — don't feed silence to Whisper
  1163→
  1164→    segments, _ = whisper_model.transcribe(audio, language="en",
  1165→                                           beam_size=1, best_of=1,
  1166→                                           initial_prompt=_whisper_prompt)
  1167→    text = " ".join(s.text for s in segments if s.no_speech_prob < 0.6).strip()
  1168→
  1169→    if _HALLUCINATIONS.search(text):
  1170→        print(f"(hallucination filtered: '{text[:60]}')")
  1171→        return ""
  1172→
  1173→    return text

  1179→def _find_chat_input(target_subname: str = "") -> object:
  1180→    subname = target_subname or VSCODE_TITLE
  1181→    vscode  = auto.WindowControl(searchDepth=1, SubName=subname)
  1182→    if not vscode.Exists(2):
  1183→        return None
  1184→
  1185→    ctrl = vscode.EditControl(searchDepth=12, Name=_CHAT_INPUT_HINT)
  1186→    if ctrl.Exists(2):
  1187→        return ctrl
  1188→
  1189→    chrome = vscode.PaneControl(searchDepth=12,
  1190→                                ClassName="Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND")
  1191→    if not chrome.Exists(2):
  1192→        return None
  1193→
  1194→    found: list[tuple[int, object]] = []
  1195→
  1196→    def _walk(ctrl, d=0):
  1197→        if d > 15:
  1198→            return
  1199→        try:
  1200→            if ctrl.ControlTypeName == "EditControl":
  1201→                found.append((d, ctrl))
  1202→        except Exception:
  1203→            pass
  1204→        try:
  1205→            child = ctrl.GetFirstChildControl()
  1206→            while child:
  1207→                _walk(child, d + 1)
  1208→                child = child.GetNextSiblingControl()
  1209→        except Exception:
  1210→            pass
  1211→
  1212→    _walk(chrome)
  1213→
  1214→    if not found:
  1215→        return None
  1216→    for _, c in found:
  1217→        if (c.Name or "") in (_CHAT_INPUT_HINT, "Message input"):
  1218→            return c
  1219→    return min(found, key=lambda x: x[0])[1]

  1311→def clean_for_speech(text: str) -> str:
  1312→    text = re.sub(r"```[\s\S]*?```", "See the chat for the code.", text)
  1313→    text = re.sub(r"`([^`]+)`", r"\1", text)
  1314→    text = re.sub(r"#{1,6}\s+", "", text)
  1315→    text = re.sub(r"\*{1,3}([^*\n]+)\*{1,3}", r"\1", text)
  1316→    text = re.sub(r"\[([^\]]+)\]\([^)]+\)", r"\1", text)
  1317→    text = re.sub(r"\n[-*_]{3,}\n", " ", text)
  1318→    text = re.sub(r"\n\s*[-*•]\s+", ". ", text)
  1319→    text = re.sub(r"\n\s*\d+\.\s+", ". ", text)
  1320→    text = re.sub(r"\n+", " ", text)
  1321→    text = re.sub(r"\s{2,}", " ", text)
  1322→    words = text.split()
  1323→    if len(words) > MAX_SPEECH_WORDS:
  1324→        text = " ".join(words[:MAX_SPEECH_WORDS]) + ". See the chat for the full response."
  1325→    return text.strip()

  1328→# ── Text-to-speech ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1329→
  1330→async def speak(text: str) -> None:
  1331→    """Speak text via Kokoro (local) or Edge TTS (fallback). Mic muted during playback."""
  1332→    _stop_speech.clear()
  1333→    _tts_active.set()
  1334→    _mic_muted.set()
  1335→    try:
  1336→        await asyncio.wait_for(_speak_save(text), timeout=25.0)
  1337→    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
  1338→        print("[Cyrus] TTS timed out — aborting playback")
  1339→        _stop_speech.set()   # signal _speak_kokoro's _play() loop to exit
  1340→    finally:
  1341→        _tts_active.clear()
  1342→        await asyncio.sleep(0.25)   # brief echo-decay guard; VAD + RMS gate cover the rest
  1343→        _mic_muted.clear()
  1344→
  1345→
  1346→async def _speak_save(text: str) -> None:
  1347→    """Synthesise and play text. Uses Kokoro if loaded, otherwise falls back to Edge TTS."""
  1348→    if _kokoro is not None:
  1349→        await _speak_kokoro(text)
  1350→    else:
  1351→        await _speak_edge(text)
  1352→
  1353→
  1354→async def _speak_kokoro(text: str) -> None:
  1355→    """Kokoro local TTS — ~80–150 ms on GPU, no network, no temp file."""
  1356→    print(f"[TTS] generating {len(text)} chars...")
  1357→    samples, sr = await asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor(
  1358→        None,
  1359→        lambda: _kokoro.create(text, voice=TTS_VOICE, speed=TTS_SPEED, lang="en-us"),
  1360→    )
  1361→    print(f"[TTS] {len(samples)} samples at {sr}Hz — playing")
  1362→
  1363→    def _play():
  1364→        chunk = 1024
  1365→        with sd.OutputStream(samplerate=sr, channels=1, dtype="float32") as stream:
  1366→            for i in range(0, len(samples), chunk):
  1367→                if _stop_speech.is_set():
  1368→                    break
  1369→                stream.write(samples[i:i + chunk].reshape(-1, 1))
  1370→
  1371→    await asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor(None, _play)
  1372→
  1373→
  1374→async def _speak_edge(text: str) -> None:
  1375→    """Edge TTS fallback — requires network + ffmpeg. Used when Kokoro is not loaded."""
  1376→    import subprocess, tempfile, edge_tts
  1377→    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".mp3", delete=False) as f:
  1378→        tmp_path = f.name
  1379→    try:
  1380→        await edge_tts.Communicate(text, "en-US-BrianNeural",
  1381→                                   rate="-5%", pitch="-5Hz").save(tmp_path)
  1382→        if not os.path.getsize(tmp_path):
  1383→            return
  1384→
  1385→        result = await asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor(
  1386→            None,
  1387→            lambda: subprocess.run(
  1388→                ["ffmpeg", "-loglevel", "quiet",
  1389→                 "-i", tmp_path,
  1390→                 "-f", "s16le", "-ar", "24000", "-ac", "1", "-"],
  1391→                capture_output=True,
  1392→            ),
  1393→        )
  1394→        pcm = result.stdout
  1395→        if not pcm:
  1396→            return
  1397→
  1398→        audio = np.frombuffer(pcm, dtype=np.int16).astype(np.float32) / 32768.0
  1399→
  1400→        def _play():
  1401→            chunk = 1024
  1402→            with sd.OutputStream(samplerate=24000, channels=1, dtype="float32") as stream:
  1403→                for i in range(0, len(audio), chunk):
  1404→                    if _stop_speech.is_set():
  1405→                        break
  1406→                    stream.write(audio[i:i + chunk])
  1407→
  1408→        await asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor(None, _play)
  1409→    finally:
  1410→        try:
  1411→            os.unlink(tmp_path)
  1412→        except (PermissionError, FileNotFoundError):
  1413→            pass

  1591→            _stop_speech.set()  # interrupt current playback
  1592→            _tts_pending.clear()
  1593→            await asyncio.sleep(0.15)  # let _speak_save stop cleanly
  1594→
  1595→        # Permission dialog — binary response, no LLM needed
  1596→        handled = False
  1597→        for pw in session_mgr.perm_watchers:
  1598→            if pw.is_pending:
  1599→                if pw.handle_response(text):
  1600→                    handled = True
  1601→                    break
  1602→        if handled:
  1603→            continue
  1604→
  1605→        # Input-box prompt — strip wake word if present, route text directly
  1606→        for pw in session_mgr.perm_watchers:
  1607→            if pw.prompt_pending:
  1608→                ws = text.lower().strip().split()
  1609→                if ws and ws[0].rstrip(",.!?") in WAKE_WORDS:
  1610→                    parts = text.split(None, 1)
  1611→                    text  = parts[1].lstrip(", ").strip() if len(parts) > 1 else text
  1612→                if pw.handle_prompt_response(text):
  1613→                    handled = True
  1614→                    break
  1615→        if handled:
  1616→            continue
  1617→
  1618→        # Wake word check — skipped when Cyrus is mid-conversation
  1619→        words = text.lower().strip().split()
  1620→        first = words[0].rstrip(",.!?") if words else ""
  1621→
  1622→        if _conversation_active:
  1623→            # In conversation — no wake word required.
  1624→            # If the user said it anyway, strip it so it doesn't confuse the LLM.
  1625→            if first in WAKE_WORDS:
  1626→                rest = text.split(None, 1)
  1627→                text = rest[1].lstrip(", ").strip() if len(rest) > 1 else ""
  1628→            if not text:
  1629→                continue
  1630→            print(f"[conversation] heard: '{text}'")
  1631→        else:
  1632→            if first not in WAKE_WORDS:
  1633→                print(f"(ignored — say 'Cyrus, ...' | heard: '{first}')")
  1634→                continue
  1635→            rest = text.split(None, 1)
  1636→            text = rest[1].lstrip(", ").strip() if len(rest) > 1 else ""
  1637→            if not text:
  1638→                # Wake word with no command — user likely paused between wake word
  1639→                # and the actual command (common when emphasising the wake word).
  1640→                # Wait up to 3 s for the next VAD chunk and treat it as the command.
  1641→                print("(wake word — listening for command...)", end=" ", flush=True)
  1642→                play_listen_chime()
  1643→                try:
  1644→                    follow_audio = await asyncio.wait_for(utterance_queue.get(), timeout=6.0)
  1645→                    text = await loop.run_in_executor(
  1646→                        _whisper_executor, transcribe, whisper_model, follow_audio
  1647→                    )
  1648→                    # Strip a repeated wake word if the user said it again
  1649→                    fw = text.lower().strip().split()
  1650→                    if fw and fw[0].rstrip(",.!?") in WAKE_WORDS:
  1651→                        parts = text.split(None, 1)
  1652→                        text  = parts[1].lstrip(", ").strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ""
  1653→                    if not text:
  1654→                        print("(no command heard)")
  1655→                        continue
  1656→                    print(f"'{text}'")
  1657→                except asyncio.TimeoutError:
  1658→                    print("(no command heard)")
  1659→                    continue
  1660→
  1661→        # ── Route utterance ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1662→        # Fast local pre-filter (commands never need the remote brain)
  1663→        decision = _fast_command(text)
  1664→
  1665→        if decision is None:
  1666→            with _active_project_lock:
  1667→                proj = _active_project
  1668→
  1669→            if _remote_url:
  1670→                # Remote mode: send text + context to the brain, fall back locally
  1671→                last_resp = session_mgr.last_response(proj)
  1672→                decision  = await _remote_route(text, proj, last_resp)
  1673→
  1674→            if decision is None:
  1675→                # Local routing (default, or remote fallback)
  1676→                if _is_answer_request(text):
  1677→                    resp = session_mgr.last_response(proj) or "I don't have a recent response to share."
  1678→                    words = resp.split()
  1679→                    if len(words) > 30:
  1680→                        resp = " ".join(words[:30]) + ". See the chat for more."
  1681→                    decision = {"action": "answer", "spoken": resp, "message": "", "command": {}}
  1682→                else:
  1683→                    decision = {"action": "forward", "message": text, "spoken": "", "command": {}}
  1684→
  1685→        action = decision.get("action", "forward")
  1686→
  1687→        if action == "answer":
  1688→            spoken = decision.get("spoken", "")
  1689→            if spoken:
  1690→                print(f"\n[Cyrus answers] {spoken[:80]}{'...' if len(spoken) > 80 else ''}")
  1691→                asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(_tts_queue.put(("", spoken)), loop)
  1692→                _conversation_active = False  # require wake word again to prevent TTS echo loop
  1693→            else:
  1694→                _conversation_active = False
  1695→
  1696→        elif action == "command":
  1697→            ctype = decision.get("command", {}).get("type", "")
  1698→            spoken = decision.get("spoken", "")
  1699→            print(f"\n[Cyrus command] {ctype}")

  1701→                                   spoken, session_mgr, loop)
  1702→            # Stay in conversation only if Cyrus asked a follow-up question

  1704→
  1705→        else:  # "forward"
  1706→            # Remote brain already appended VOICE_HINT; local mode has not
  1707→            raw_msg = _strip_fillers(decision.get("message") or text)
  1708→            message = raw_msg if _remote_url else raw_msg + VOICE_HINT
  1709→            with _active_project_lock:
  1710→                proj = _active_project

  1713→                submitted = await asyncio.wait_for(
  1714→                    loop.run_in_executor(None, submit_to_vscode, message),
  1715→                    timeout=8.0,
  1716→                )

  1718→                print("→ Submit timed out — VS Code UIA unresponsive.\n")
  1719→                submitted = False
  1720→            if not submitted:
  1721→                print("→ Could not find VS Code window.\n")
  1722→            else:
  1723→                play_chime()   # instant "I got it" signal while Claude processes
  1724→            # Sent to Claude Code — conversation with Cyrus ends
  1725→            _conversation_active = False
  1726→
  1727→
  1728→if __name__ == "__main__":
  1729→    try:
  1730→        asyncio.run(main())
  1731→    except KeyboardInterrupt:
  1732→        pass

  1734→        print("\nCyrus signing off.")

  1736→        _stop_speech.set()    # aborts any in-progress TTS playback
  1737→        _mic_muted.set()      # stops VAD from processing new frames

  1739→            pygame.mixer.stop()   # stop all playing sounds before tearing down SDL
  1740→            pygame.mixer.quit()

  1744→            sd.stop()             # abort all PortAudio streams
  1745→        except Exception:
